item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license as it's a direct still from a Baby Einsteins video, which would make it copyrightable to Baby Einsteins. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Corruptions of children's media - especially things as bizarre as Baby Mozart - are great sources for underlying creepiness. I enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's hard not to like him eventually. He is adorable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it scared me too

wait a minute

i also left the room when he appeared (i think i liked him after a while)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: :) Thanks again

item.title: Re: vhs artifaced verison of image?
item.content:encoded: P.S. your sock puppet profile pic is great. Feels very fitting.

item.title: Re: vhs artifaced verison of image?
item.content:encoded: Thank you!!!

item.title: Re: vhs artifaced verison of image?
item.content:encoded: Agghhh it's perfect!!! Yeah I'll totally use this. Thanks!

item.title: vhs artifaced verison of image?
item.content:encoded:
I have a question, would you like a more VHS artifacted verison of the image you used since the SCP is about a VHS tape?

I made it using a glitch app from the App Store that’s known for being authentic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah!!! That always got to me when I was little for whatever reason. It got to the point that I'd leave the room because I was so scared of it.

Now I just think it's adorable, though. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know right?? It scared the hell out of me as a kid though. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I wanted to make it clear regardless, a few others who read it didn't seem to think it was very obvious. Anyways, yes, the singular ones do spell out letters. You can decode them if you want. :) btw let me know if I screwed up the Morse sequences; I had to fill in every single time stamp and code so I may have made a mistake somewhere XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dang, that is really nice. I like the story, and the questions surrounding it (basically: what the hell is actually happening here?) Very sweet, almost unexpectedly so. Do the singular dots and dashes spell anything?

(0.25-second signal representing “dots” and 1.02-second signals representing “dashes”)

One criticism, I don't think you need this, the relationship is apparent from the way you've written the preceding sentence. Otherwise, I have no complaints. :) +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
the puppet dragon was the best part

walks into screen
looks around
faces the viewers

BLAHHH!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for letting me know!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you guys so much for the nice reviews! It really means a lot to me that you seemed to like this skip, like seriously. Thanks a million.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's basically the same thing. Mozart music plays in an infinite black void while pictures of toys and stuff are shown. Highly intellectual content.

All joking aside, my sister loved it as a kid, and so did I (but the puppet dragon always scared me XD).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is baby mozart similar to baby einstien? because my mother let me watch those when I was young.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yay it’s posted! You already know how much I love this darn thing, but imma say it again.

I love this skip.

Take my heckin’ upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really loved this - there’s something both comforting and eerie about an anomalous entity that isn’t even sure what it is or what it’s experiencing. Almost as if we’re all lost - both ourselves and the SCP. This is a great example of that and gets a +1 from me. Please keep up the good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
holy crap baby mozart
i remember that
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That…. image is really cute.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love weird, safe SCP's, and this is certainly that. The transcript is well put together and doesn't outlast its welcome, and despite the oddly presented 'conversation' was easy enough to follow. This is a cool concept, and you've pulled it off well!

I do have some minor issues with the clinical/scientific tone at the start of the entry? 'Left on at all times' just feels a little off for me. 'SCP-3798 is to be connected to a power supply at all times' perhaps? And I feel like it could do with more specificity about the TV itself, perhaps a make and model?

Those minor nitpicks aside, I really like it! Easy +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glad to see it come to fruition.
It was great as a draft, and it's great as an SCP
Have a +1
